Paper / documentation title: CLOSER/ALSPAC gambling questionnaire records
Notes: Use for variable-level extraction

Paper / documentation title: APMS 2023/24 Gambling behaviour chapter
Notes: Recent adult mental-health dataset

Paper / documentation title: Emerging Adults Gambling Survey protocol/publication
Notes: Useful for extraction of constructs

Paper / documentation title: Suicidality and gambling among young adults in Great Britain
Notes: Demonstrates use of EAGS for mental health-gambling analysis

Paper / documentation title: The Emerging Adults Gambling Survey: study protocol
Risk/protective factors analysed: Digital technology/gaming/social media and young adults
Notes: Uploaded protocol confirms ages 16-24, Wave 1 n=3549 and Wave 2 planned; useful source for study design.

Paper / documentation title: The relationship between problematic gambling severity and engagement with gambling products
Gambling measure used: Problem Gambling Severity Index (PGSI); 19 gambling activities
Risk/protective factors analysed: Eysenck Impulsivity Scale; socio-demographic/economic characteristics; gambling involvement
Notes: Uploaded paper demonstrates longitudinal analysis of young adults aged 16-26.

Paper / documentation title: ESPAD Report 2019 / 2024 data portal
Gambling measure used: Gambling for money / online gambling / problematic gambling
Notes: Cross-national school survey

Paper / documentation title: Gambling Impact and Behavior Study, 1997â€“1999
Notes: Historical US youth/adult data

Paper / documentation title: Adolescent online gambling in Growing Up in Ireland
Notes: Confirms waves 3 and 4 gambling variables

Paper / documentation title: Health Survey for England 2021 Gambling report
Notes: Confirms PGSI

Paper / documentation title: Cross Validation of the Gambling Problem Severity Subscale
Notes: Confirms CAGI/GPSS use

Paper / documentation title: Traumatic brain injuries and problem gambling in youth
Notes: Confirms gambling analyses in OSDUHS

Paper / documentation title: Scottish Health Survey 2017/2018/2019/2021 Gambling chapter
Notes: Confirms PGSI

Paper / documentation title: Gambling among adolescents in Wales / SHRN data paper
Notes: Data profile confirms SHRN resource

Paper / documentation title: The Mental Health and Well-Being of Ontario Students, 1991â€“2023: Findings from the Ontario Student Drug Use and Health Survey
Gambling measure used: Online gambling; gambling while playing a video game/loot boxes/in-game currency/skins betting; gambling in other ways
Risk/protective factors analysed: Mental health, physical health, bullying, cyberbullying, video gaming, social media use, school climate, sex, grade, region
Notes: Confirms 2023 OSDUHS includes grades 7-12 with N=10,145 students and gambling/digital behaviour indicators.

Paper / documentation title: Patterns of Gambling and Other Risky Behaviors among Adolescents. A Latent Class Analysis Study on the 2021/22 Health Behaviors in School Children Survey Data
Gambling measure used: Any gambling in last 12 months; South Oaks Gambling Screenâ€“Revised for Adolescents (SOGS-RA)
Risk/protective factors analysed: Tobacco, alcohol, drunkenness, cannabis, other substance use, self-rated health, Cantril Ladder life satisfaction, sex, age cohort, school type
Notes: Confirms HBSC Lombardy 2021/22 gambling data among ages 15 and 17; 11- and 13-year-olds excluded because gambling and other health behaviour data were not available.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling, Saving, and Lumpy Liquidity Needs
Gambling measure used: Sports betting demand, targeted payouts, winnings
Notes: American Economic Journal: Applied Economics 13(1), 72-104. Replication data at openICPSR project 115162.

Paper / documentation title: Open data on health-related neighbourhood features in Great Britain
Risk/protective factors analysed: Built environment, retail hazards, health services, green/blue space, air quality
Notes: AHAH v5 released February 2026. Composite index covering ~1.7M postcodes at LSOA/Data Zone level.

Paper / documentation title: CLOSER written evidence to UK Parliament on social and economic impact of the gambling industry
Notes: Cites BCS70 age-16 betting/gambling spending item: around 6,000 respondents, 5% spent money on betting/gambling, and 5% of those saw it as their most important spending activity; notes the question was not repeated. Policy evidence, not a peer-reviewed gambling analysis paper.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling in Young Adults Aged 17-24 Years: A Population-Based Study
Notes: Uses ALSPAC gambling surveys at ages 17, 20 and 24 to examine participation, regular gambling and associated child, parental and socioeconomic factors.

Paper / documentation title: Problem Gambling in Early Adulthood: a Population-Based Study
Notes: Uses ALSPAC PGSI data at ages 20 and 24 to examine stability, antecedents and consequences of problem gambling.

Paper / documentation title: Transmission of Problem Gambling Between Adjacent Generations
Notes: Uses ALSPAC to examine links between parental gambling during childhood and offspring problem gambling at age 20.

Paper / documentation title: A longitudinal study of gambling in late adolescence and early adulthood: the ALSPAC Gambling Study
Notes: GambleAware/Bristol summary using ALSPAC ages 17-24; reports gambling participation, regular gambling, problem gambling and risk factors.

Paper / documentation title: Religious Background and Gambling Among Young Adults in the United States
Notes: Uses the National Longitudinal Study of Adolescent to Adult Health to examine adolescent religious background and young-adult gambling behaviour/problems.

Paper / documentation title: Gender differences in genetic and environmental influences on gambling: results from a sample of twins from the National Longitudinal Study of Adolescent Health
Notes: Uses the Add Health twin sample to examine genetic and environmental influences on gambling behaviour.

Paper / documentation title: The relationship of ADHD symptoms to gambling behaviour in the USA: Results from the National Longitudinal Study of Adolescent Health
Notes: Uses Add Health data to examine associations between retrospectively reported ADHD symptoms and young-adult gambling behaviour/disordered gambling characteristics.

Paper / documentation title: The association between problem gambling and psychotic experiences: Findings from the Adult Psychiatric Morbidity Survey 2007
Notes: Uses APMS 2007 gambling data and psychosis screening measures to examine associations between at-risk/problem gambling and psychotic experiences.

Paper / documentation title: Estimated verbal IQ and the odds of problem gambling: a population-based study
Notes: Uses APMS 2007 to examine whether lower estimated verbal IQ is associated with problem gambling.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling, geographical variations and deprivation: findings from the adult psychiatric morbidity survey
Notes: Uses APMS gambling data to examine geographical variation and deprivation patterns in gambling problems.

Paper / documentation title: Problem gambling and suicidal thoughts, suicide attempts and non-suicidal self-harm in England: evidence from the Adult Psychiatric Morbidity Survey 2007
Notes: GambleAware report using APMS 2007 to examine problem gambling, suicidal thoughts, suicide attempts and non-suicidal self-harm.

Paper / documentation title: Exploring problem gambling, loneliness and lifetime suicidal behaviours: a cross-sectional study using the Adult Psychiatric Morbidity Survey 2007
Authors / year: John, Lee, Wardle, McManus and Dymond, 2019
Notes: GambleAware report using APMS 2007 to examine problem gambling, loneliness and suicidal behaviours.

Paper / documentation title: What proportion of gambling is problem gambling? Estimates from the 2010 British Gambling Prevalence Survey
Notes: Secondary analysis of British Gambling Prevalence Survey 2010 data.

Paper / documentation title: British gambling prevalence survey 2010
Notes: Official BGPS 2010 report; nationally representative data on gambling participation, problem gambling and gambling attitudes in Great Britain.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling behaviour in Great Britain in 2016
Authors / year: Conolly, Wardle, Davies, Fuller and Heinze, 2018
Notes: Uses combined Health Survey for England, Scottish Health Survey and Wales Omnibus data to report gambling participation and problem gambling in Great Britain.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling behaviour in Great Britain in 2015
Authors / year: Conolly, Fuller, Jones, Maplethorpe, Sondaal and Wardle, 2017
Notes: Uses Health Survey for England / Scottish Health Survey combined gambling data plus Wales Omnibus data.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling behaviour in England and Scotland: Findings from the Health Survey for England 2012 and Scottish Health Survey 2012
Notes: Uses HSE 2012 and SHeS 2012 gambling modules to examine gambling and at-risk/problem gambling.

Paper / documentation title: The cards they're dealt: types of gambling activity, online gambling, and risk of problem gambling in European adolescents
Notes: Uses ESPAD 2019 data from 85,420 students aged 16 across 33 countries to examine gambling type, online gambling and problem gambling risk.

Paper / documentation title: The Multiplicative Effect of Combining Alcohol with Energy Drinks on Adolescent Gambling
Notes: Uses ESPAD Italia 2015 to examine alcohol mixed with energy drinks and at-risk/problem gambling among adolescents.

Paper / documentation title: Odds of gambling among adolescents: a secondary analysis of the cross-sectional European School Survey Project on Alcohol and Drugs
Notes: Uses Irish ESPAD 2019 data to examine factors associated with gambling and excessive gambling among 15-16 year olds.

Paper / documentation title: A large-scale national study of gambling severity among immigrant and non-immigrant adolescents: The role of the family
Notes: Uses Italian HBSC 2013/14 data to examine immigrant status, family characteristics and adolescent gambling severity.

Paper / documentation title: Prevalence of gambling behaviours and their associations with socioemotional harm among 11-16 year olds in Wales: findings from the School Health Research Network survey
Notes: Uses 2017 SHRN Student Health and Wellbeing Survey gambling questions from 37,363 students in 193 Welsh secondary schools.

Paper / documentation title: A Longitudinal Study of the Temporal Relation Between Problem Gambling and Mental and Substance Use Disorders Among Young Adults
Notes: Uses the Manitoba Longitudinal Study of Young Adults to examine temporal relations between at-risk/problem gambling and mental/substance-use disorders.

Paper / documentation title: The Temporal Relationship Between Faulty Gambling Cognitions and Gambling Severity in Young Adults
Notes: Uses Manitoba Longitudinal Survey of Young Adults data to examine gambling cognitions and gambling severity over time.

Paper / documentation title: Cross Validation of the Gambling Problem Severity Subscale of the Canadian Adolescent Gambling Index (CAGI/GPSS) on a Sample of Ontario High School Students
Notes: Uses OSDUHS 2015 data from Ontario high school students to validate the CAGI/GPSS gambling problem severity subscale.

Paper / documentation title: Traumatic brain injuries and problem gambling in youth: Evidence from a population-based study of secondary students in Ontario, Canada
Notes: Uses OSDUHS 2011, 2013 and 2015 cycles to examine traumatic brain injury and problem gambling among adolescents.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling on the Lottery: Sociodemographic Correlates Across the Lifespan
Notes: SAMHSA/CBHSQ record identifies NSDUH use for a Journal of Gambling Studies paper on lottery gambling across the lifespan.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling in children and adolescents
Notes: Review using ALSPAC and Gambling Commission data on prevalence, risk factors, online gambling. PMID 32932525.

Paper / documentation title: Predicting disordered gambling across adolescence and young adulthood from polygenic contributions to Big 5 personality traits
Notes: Polygenic scores for Big 5 traits and disordered gambling at ages 17, 20, 24. PMID 34342067.

Paper / documentation title: The relationship between PGSI scores and suicidality: Results of a 9-year cohort study
Notes: PGSI and suicide attempts at ages 24-25; longitudinal 1-5 year prediction. PMID 40957451.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling by Young Adults in the UK During COVID-19 Lockdown
Notes: Gambling frequency and activities during first COVID-19 lockdown in May 2020. PMID 33997939.

Paper / documentation title: Adolescent body dissatisfaction and disordered eating: Predictors of later risky health behaviours
Notes: Body dissatisfaction at 14 predicting gambling and other risky behaviours at 21. PMID 31450163.

Paper / documentation title: Externalizing as a common genetic influence for substance use and behavioral conditions: A developmental perspective
Notes: Externalizing polygenic risk scores predicting gambling alongside substance use across ages. PMID 40767372.

Paper / documentation title: Multiple risk behaviour in adolescence is associated with substantial adverse health and social outcomes
Notes: Multiple risk behaviours at age 16 predicting problem gambling at age 18. PMID 32473267.

Paper / documentation title: Early-life maternal attachment and risky health behaviours in adolescence
Notes: Maternal attachment at 9 months predicting gambling at age 17. Only genuine MCS gambling paper (20 others use Cambridge Gambling Task). PMID 34749702.

Paper / documentation title: The association between health-related quality of life and problem gambling severity
Notes: EQ-5D health utilities and PGSI categories using HSE data. PMID 38347455.

Paper / documentation title: The association between gambling frequency and risk of harm: Analysis using health survey data from England and Scotland
Notes: Gambling frequency and PGSI scores across six waves of HSE and SHeS. PMID 41700531.

Paper / documentation title: The prevalence and clustering of alcohol consumption, gambling, smoking, and excess weight in an English adult population
Notes: Clustering of at-risk gambling with drinking, smoking, overweight in HSE 2012-2018. PMID 37633599.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling and wellbeing: Uneven gains and deficits across risk levels
Notes: Subjective wellbeing across gambling risk spectrum in HSE. PMID 41633232.

Paper / documentation title: Exploring the impact on emotional wellbeing of having a spouse with elevated PGSI scores
Notes: Partner PGSI scores and individual emotional wellbeing using HSE and SHeS. PMID 40899112.

Paper / documentation title: The association between increasing levels of gambling harm and emotional health outcomes below the threshold of disordered gambling
Notes: PGSI scores below 8 and mental health/wellbeing outcomes in HSE and SHeS. PMID 40782593.

Paper / documentation title: Latent class analysis of co-morbidity in the Adult Psychiatric Morbidity Survey 2007
Notes: Problem gambling as one of 15 mental health/behavioural problems in latent class co-morbidity analysis. PMID 21375797.

Paper / documentation title: Problem Gambling in the UK: Implications for Health, Psychosocial Adjustment and Health Care Utilization
Notes: Problem gambling associations with mental/physical health, psychosocial adjustment, health care use. PMID 26343859.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling problems and the impact of family in UK armed forces veterans
Notes: Problem gambling in UK veterans vs non-veterans using APMS 2007. PMID 29739238.

Paper / documentation title: Association Between Problem Gambling and Functional Disability
Notes: Problem gambling and functional disability (ADL/IADL) in UK adults. PMID 34128488.

Paper / documentation title: Relationship between ADHD symptoms and problem gambling: A mediation analysis
Notes: ADHD symptoms and problem gambling with mediation by CMDs, BPD traits, stressful life events. PMID 30238788.

Paper / documentation title: Disordered gambling, type of gambling and gambling involvement in the BGPS 2007
Notes: Gambling types and disordered gambling; gambling involvement as predictor. PMID 19892851.

Paper / documentation title: Validation of the 8-item Attitudes Towards Gambling Scale (ATGS-8)
Notes: Psychometric validation of ATGS-8 in BGPS 2010. PMID 26722993.

Paper / documentation title: The Extent and Distribution of Gambling-Related Harms and the Prevention Paradox
Notes: Prevention paradox: gambling harms distributed across low-to-moderate risk gamblers in BGPS 2010. PMID 27156382.

Paper / documentation title: Sociodemographic correlates of internet gambling: findings from the 2007 BGPS
Notes: First national analysis of internet gamblers vs non-internet gamblers. PMID 19072080.

Paper / documentation title: Validation of the reasons for gambling questionnaire (RGQ) in a British population survey
Notes: Validation of RGQ five-factor structure in BGPS 2010. PMID 25746361.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling Motives: Application of the RGQ in an Australian Population Survey
Notes: Cross-national comparison of gambling motives using RGQ developed for BGPS 2010. PMID 24705633.

Paper / documentation title: Unraveling the Impact of Early Adversity and Perpetration on Problematic Gambling in Young Adults
Notes: Childhood adversity and problematic gambling in 16,760 young adults aged 24-32. PMID 41082064.

Paper / documentation title: A closer look at sex differences in genetic and environmental influences on gambling
Notes: Genetic vs environmental influences on gambling in Add Health twins aged 18-28. PMID 24033632.

Paper / documentation title: Confounding Bias in the Relationship Between Problem Gambling and Crime
Notes: Problem gambling and criminal behaviour; propensity score analysis in young adults. PMID 32185650.

Paper / documentation title: Risk factors for homelessness: evidence from a population-based study
Notes: Gambling addiction as one risk factor for lifetime homelessness. PMID 19339321.

Paper / documentation title: A Longitudinal Examination of Young People's Gambling Behaviours and Participation in Team Sports
Notes: Team sport participation and gambling at ages 17-20; online gambling increase from 2.6% to 9.3%. PMID 36527539.

Paper / documentation title: Frequency of gambling, mental health, and financial stress among young adults
Notes: Weekly/monthly gambling at age 20 and associations with depression, life satisfaction, loan repayment difficulties. PMID 41382047.

Paper / documentation title: Gaming and gambling behaviours among Italian adolescents: a cross-sectional study
Notes: ESPAD Italia 2023; gaming-gambling convergence; SOGS-RA; loot box spending. Lancet Public Health. PMID 41391469.

Paper / documentation title: Cross-jurisdictional factors linked to gambling frequency in adolescents from 28 European countries: a machine learning approach
Notes: Random forest ML identifying predictors of gambling frequency across 28 countries. PMID 40578225.

Paper / documentation title: Non-prescribed pharmaceutical stimulants use among adolescents
Notes: ESPAD Italia 2019; gambling as risky behaviour co-occurring with stimulant use. PMID 37549544.

Paper / documentation title: Risk Factors for Excessive Social Media Use Differ from Those of Gambling and Gaming in Finnish Youth
Notes: ESPAD 2019 Finland; excessive social media vs gambling vs gaming risk factors. PMID 35206593.

Paper / documentation title: Money used in gaming is associated with problem gambling: Results of the ESPAD 2019 Finland
Notes: In-game spending and loot boxes as risk for gambling; parental monitoring protective. PMID 34797777.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling among 16-year-olds and associated covariates: A Nordic comparison
Notes: Nordic cross-country gambling comparison using ESPAD 2015. PMID 32522086.

Paper / documentation title: Problem gambling among Czech adolescents: An exploration of its relationship to early initiation of tobacco smoking
Notes: ESPAD Czech Republic 2015; early daily smoking onset doubles risk of problem gambling. PMID 30704262.

Paper / documentation title: Prevalence of youth gambling and potential influence of substance use and other risk factors throughout 33 European countries
Notes: 22.6% of 16-year-olds gambled past year across 33 European countries. ESPAD 2015. PMID 29806197.

Paper / documentation title: I Am Becoming More and More Like My Eldest Brother!: Older siblings, adolescent gambling severity, and parental role
Notes: ESPAD Italia 2012; older gambling siblings increase at-risk/problem gambling. PMID 27718036.

Paper / documentation title: Adolescent Gambling-Oriented Attitudes Mediate the Relationship Between Perceived Parental Knowledge and Adolescent Gambling
Notes: ESPAD Italia 2012; parental knowledge reduces gambling via adolescent attitudes. PMID 27448214.

Paper / documentation title: Country- and individual-level determinants of probable problematic gambling in adolescence
Notes: ESPAD 2011 multi-level analysis across 9 European countries; parental caring protective. PMID 25171305.

Paper / documentation title: Methodological background and descriptive features of the ESPAD 2011 Study in Hungary
Notes: Hungarian ESPAD 2011 methodology; gambling included as behavioural addiction measure. PMID 23689434.

Paper / documentation title: The effects of social isolation and problematic social media use on well-being in a sample of young Italian gamblers
Notes: HBSC Italy 2021/22 Tuscany; social isolation, social media, wellbeing across gambler types. PMID 41235053.

Paper / documentation title: A Southern addiction evaluation project: investigating the impact of addiction through a survey HBSC based
Notes: HBSC-based survey in Southern Italy (Messina); gambling prevalence decreasing. PMID 38571473.

Paper / documentation title: Should I give kids money? The role of pocket money on at-risk behaviors in Italian adolescents
Notes: HBSC Italy 2018 Lombardy; pocket money associated with gambling among 15-year-olds. PMID 36974703.

Paper / documentation title: Alcohol use and misuse: a profile of adolescents from 2018 Italian HBSC data
Notes: HBSC Italy 2018; 21% heavy drinkers cluster also had 52% gambling experience. PMID 33346182.

Paper / documentation title: Relative deprivation and disordered gambling in youths
Notes: HBSC Italy 2013/14; relative deprivation linked to 4x increase in disordered gambling symptoms. PMID 29514922.

Paper / documentation title: Income Inequality and Adolescent Gambling Severity: Findings from a Large-Scale Italian Representative Survey
Notes: HBSC Italy 2013/14; regional income inequality predicts gambling severity; North-South gradient. PMID 28824499.

Paper / documentation title: Is Medicine Use for Nervousness Associated with Adolescent At-Risk or Problem Gambling?
Notes: HBSC Italy 2013/14; medicine use for nervousness 3x odds of at-risk/problem gambling. PMID 28797002.

Paper / documentation title: Childhood ADHD and addictive behaviours in adolescence: a Canadian sample
Notes: ADHD youth non-significant trend toward more gambling; OSDUHS as population comparison. PMID 24872828.

Paper / documentation title: Co-development of Problem Gambling and Depression Symptoms in Emerging Adults
Notes: MLSYA 4 waves; 5 joint trajectory classes of depression and problem gambling. PMID 29468344.

Paper / documentation title: Investigating Possible Reciprocal Relations Between Depressive and Problem Gambling Symptoms in Emerging Adults
Notes: MLSYA 4 waves; depression and problem gambling correlated but neither is a risk factor for the other. PMID 27253700.

Paper / documentation title: Validation of the Gambling Motives Questionnaire in Emerging Adults
Notes: GMQ three-factor structure validated; enhancement motives predict problem gambling independently. PMID 24871297.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling, smoking and alcohol use in Great Britain: insights from the 2023 Gambling Survey of Great Britain
Notes: GSGB 2023 (n=9,742); PGSI dose-dependent association with AUDIT-C and smoking. PMID 41690257.

Paper / documentation title: Gambling products, gambling problems and gambling involvement: comparative analysis from GSGB and HSE
Notes: GSGB 2023 + HSE 2015-2018; online casino and EGMs independently associated with elevated PGSI. PMID 40966826.

Paper / documentation title: Past-year recreational gambling in a nationally representative sample: correlates of casino, non-casino, and both casino/non-casino gambling
Notes: GIBS (n=2,417); casino vs non-casino recreational gamblers. PMID 21550124.

Paper / documentation title: Income differences and recreational gambling
Notes: GIBS 1998 (n=2,417); income differences in recreational gambling behaviour. PMID 21768950.

Paper / documentation title: Gender differences in adolescent gambling
Notes: GIBS 1998 (n=534 adolescents); gender differences in gambling and psychiatric comorbidity. PMID 16402759.

Paper / documentation title: The influence of religiosity on gambling participation
Notes: GIBS/NGISC 1999 data; religious participation negatively associated with gambling. PMID 16826457.
